articles.title: Horoscope for Thursday, April 23, 2026
articles.description:
Moon Alert

Avoid shopping (except food and gas) and important decisions from 2 p.m. to 3 p.m. After that, the moon moves from Cancer into Leo.

Aries (March 21-April 19)

A positive day

Keep your eyes open because it's possible you can get richer today. It might be in a tiny way, but it could be in a bigger way. (One can only hope.) The bottom line is that unexpected gifts, goodies and favors from others will come your way today. Keep your pockets open!

Taurus (April 20-May 20)

A positive day

This is a favorable day because the sun, Venus and Uranus are all in Taurus, and your ruler Venus is lined up with unpredictable Uranus. Unexpected events and exciting new experiences might occur, especially through love and romance. Perhaps a new relationship with someone unusual?

Gemini (May 21-June 20)

An average day

This is probably a restless day for you. Even though you feel keen to talk to others, and you might also feel competitive, you're a bit unsure of your direction. Unusual money-making ideas might occur to you. Spontaneous ways to spend your money might also occur. (Keep your receipts.)

Cancer (June 21-July 22)

A positive day

This is the classic day to meet someone unusual — perhaps a friend or an acquaintance in a group setting. This person could be a romantic interest, or not. Quite likely, they will introduce something unusual or unexpected into your world.

Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)

A positive day

Today you might encounter an unusual authority figure. ("Hi Darth.") Quite possibly, this conversation will make you feel restless, but it might also inspire you in some way. An unexpected advantage — even money — might be the result. Stay open to all possibilities.

Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)

A positive day

Unexpected opportunities to travel might fall in your lap today. If so, act quickly because this window of opportunity will be brief. Someone unusual might enter your world, introducing you to new ideas, new knowledge and a greater sense of freedom.

Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)

An average day

You're high-viz today! People notice you more than usual. Quite possibly, something unexpected will occur that causes this. Certainly, this is the kind of day where you can benefit from the wealth and resources of someone else. Don't hesitate — say yes.

Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)

An average day

Don't be surprised if someone close to you makes an unusual suggestion today. They might have news about potential travel plans or something to do with higher education, medicine or the law. Whatever it is will likely please you. (You will have to make a fast decision.)

Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)

An average day

Your work routine will be interrupted today by something unexpected, but it could be pleasant. New equipment, unexpected assistance, work-related travel or unusual people appearing in your world are just some examples. Stay on your toes!

Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)

An average day

A new romance or an unexpected flirtation with someone who is "different" might occur today. Meanwhile, today holds all kinds of fun-loving opportunities. Accept invitations to socialize, especially if they involve learning something new or meeting new people. Live it up!

Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)

An average day

Stock the fridge because spontaneous get-togethers and surprise entertaining might occur for you. This could be related to your work or something to do with a pet. You might also buy something beautiful, artistic or high-tech for where you live. It’s an interesting day!

Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)

An average day

Keep your wits about you today because this is a mildly accident-prone day. However, it's an exciting day because you will see new places, meet new faces and have thrilling new ideas. New romance or fun, unexpected outings will delight. Parents should keep an eye on their children to avoid accidents.

If today is your birthday

Actor and wrestler John Cena (1977) shares your birthday today. You're compassionate, intellectual and inquisitive. You enjoy talking to people and have a strong sense of justice. This is a year of new beginnings, adventures and major changes. Keep your eyes open for new opportunities. Take action and initiative and define your goals. Prepare for leadership.

articles.title: Scientists make breakthrough in solving mystery of volcanic lightning
articles.description: Research shows fine carbon coating on silica in updraught causes electrical charge – and spectacular display Researchers are a step closer to understanding volcanic lightning, one of the most spectacular atmospheric phenomena, which can be seen playing among the clouds of smoke and ash during an eruption. The intensity is extreme: the Hunga Tonga-Hunga Ha‘apai eruption, in the Tongan archipelago in 2022, produced more than 2,600 lightning flashes a minute stretching up to 19 miles (31km) above sea level. We know that storm clouds become electrically charged as a result of collisions between ice crystals rising in updraughts and falling particles of graupel, or soft hail. The ice picks up positive charge and the hail negative. What has puzzled scientists is how a volcanic plume, which is dry and consists of ash and rock fragments, could pick up charge. Particles made from the same rocky material should not do that during collisions. Continue reading...

This ancient crocodile relative grew up on four legs then walked on two
articles.description: A bizarre crocodile relative from the age of dinosaurs is rewriting what scientists thought they knew about ancient reptiles. This poodle-sized creature, called Sonselasuchus cedrus, appears to have started life walking on all fours before shifting to a two-legged stance as it matured—an unusual transformation rarely seen in the fossil record.

articles.title: Tropical forest ecosystems bounce back much faster than we thought — and more media coverage of UCLA
articles.description: The New York Times featured an article about a new study that found animals returned to deforested land much more quickly than scientists had long thought. UCLA’s Timo Metz, the study’s first author, says the findings disproved the notion that forest regrowth would have to precede animal reintroduction in deforested areas that had been used as pastures or cacao plantations. Instead, many animals returned within three decades rather than after centuries. Read more about UCLA in today’s Associated Press and KCBS-TV.

articles.title: WFP Somalia Country Brief Apri 2026
articles.description: Country: Somalia Source: World Food Programme Please refer to the attached file. Somalia’s food security and nutrition crisis continues to worsen, with 6.5 million people facing acute food insecurity IPC Phase 3 and above, including 2 million in Emergency IPC Phase 4, alongside critical levels of acute malnutrition affecting an estimated 1.84 million children, underscoring the scale and severity of humanitarian needs. Severe funding shortfalls are disrupting essential health and nutrition services and constraining the humanitarian response, forcing WFP to implement increasingly sharp prioritization amid rising needs. Without urgent new funding, life‐saving assistance will continue to decrease through July after which lifesaving assistance may come to a complete halt without new resources, significantly heightening the risk of worsening food insecurity and malnutrition.

articles.title: Severe storms could hit Kansas City in two waves. Here’s the timeline and threats

Damaging winds are expected to be the main hazard. Parts of Kansas, Nebraska, Iowa and Minnesota are also under a slight risk.
